description: Add a `browser_notify` browser tool that sends Chrome desktop notifications via the extension's chrome.notifications API. The tool accepts a title, message, optional icon URL, and optional requireInteraction flag. The extension receives the dispatch via WebSocket and calls chrome.notifications.create(). Clicking the notification opens the specified URL (if provided) or the side panel. Includes extensive E2E tests.

userStories.title: Add browser.showNotification handler in the extension
userStories.description: Add a new browser command handler in the extension that receives notification parameters via WebSocket and calls chrome.notifications.create(). Add the handler to the browser-commands barrel and register it in the message router.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserShowNotification exists in a new file browser-commands/notification-commands.ts
Handler calls chrome.notifications.create with type 'basic', title, message, iconUrl (defaulting to extension icon), priority, and requireInteraction
Handler supports optional contextMessage field
Handler generates a unique notification ID and returns it in the success result
Handler is exported from browser-commands/index.ts
Message router maps 'browser.showNotification' to the handler via wrapAsync
known-methods.test.ts mock includes handleBrowserShowNotification
Unit tests cover: successful creation, default icon fallback, optional fields
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/notification-commands.ts (NEW — create this file)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/notification-commands.test.ts (NEW — unit tests)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/index.ts (add export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts (register handler)
- platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.test.ts (add mock)

Context:
Browser commands follow a consistent pattern. Each handler receives `(params: Record<string, unknown>, id: string | number)` and uses `sendSuccessResult(id, ...)` or `sendErrorResult(id, err)` from messaging.ts. Example handler — `handleBrowserFocusTab` in `platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/tab-commands.ts`:
```typescript
export const handleBrowserFocusTab = async (params: Record<string, unknown>, id: string | number): Promise<void> => {
  try {
    const tabId = requireTabId(params, id);
    if (tabId === null) return;
    const tab = await chrome.tabs.update(tabId, { active: true });
    ...
    sendSuccessResult(id, { id: tab.id, title: tab.title ?? '', url: tab.url ?? '', active: true });
  } catch (err) {
    sendErrorResult(id, err);
  }
};
```

The notification-commands.ts handler should:
1. Extract `title` (required string), `message` (required string), `iconUrl` (optional string), `requireInteraction` (optional boolean, default false), `contextMessage` (optional string), `url` (optional string — stored for click handling) from params
2. Generate a unique notification ID like `opentabs-notify-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`
3. Call `chrome.notifications.create(notificationId, { type: 'basic', iconUrl: iconUrl ?? chrome.runtime.getURL('icons/icon-128.png'), title, message, contextMessage, priority: 1, requireInteraction })`
4. Return `sendSuccessResult(id, { notificationId })` on success
5. Return `sendErrorResult(id, err)` on failure
6. Store `{ notificationId, url }` in a module-level Map so the click handler can open the URL
7. Export an `initNotificationClickHandler()` function that registers `chrome.notifications.onClicked` — when clicked, if the notification has a URL, open it via `chrome.tabs.create({ url })`; otherwise open the side panel. Always clear the notification after click.

Message router registration — in `platform/browser-extension/src/message-router.ts`, the handler map is a `Map<string, MessageHandler>` built from entries array (lines ~678-730). Add:
```typescript
['browser.showNotification', wrapAsync('browser.showNotification', handleBrowserShowNotification)],
```
after the existing browser handlers. Import from `./browser-commands/index.js`.

The existing confirmation-badge.ts already uses `chrome.notifications.onClicked` with a listener that checks `notificationId === 'opentabs-confirmation'`. The new click handler in notification-commands.ts should check that the notificationId starts with `opentabs-notify-` so the two listeners don't conflict.

The `initNotificationClickHandler()` must be called from background.ts at startup, similar to how `initConfirmationBadge()` is called.

For known-methods.test.ts — add `handleBrowserShowNotification: vi.fn(asyncNoop),` to the `browser-commands/index.js` mock (around line 66, after the existing mocks).

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify confirmation-badge.ts or its notification handling
- Do NOT use the Web Notifications API — only chrome.notifications
- The notification ID prefix must be 'opentabs-notify-' to avoid collision with the confirmation notification ID 'opentabs-confirmation'
- Do NOT add initNotificationClickHandler to background.ts in this story — that's US-003's job

userStories.title: Add browser_notify tool definition on the MCP server
userStories.description: Add a new browser tool definition that dispatches to the extension's browser.showNotification handler. Register it in the browser tools barrel and regenerate the browser tools catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/show-notification.ts exists with defineBrowserTool
Tool name is 'browser_notify'

Zod input schema has: title (string, required), message (string, required), iconUrl (string, optional), requireInteraction (boolean, optional), contextMessage (string, optional), url (string, optional)
Handler dispatches to extension via dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.showNotification', args)
Tool is added to the browserTools array in browser-tools/index.ts
Tool has group 'Notifications', icon 'bell', and a descriptive summary
npm run generate:browser-tools-catalog updates the generated catalog to include browser_notify

userStories.title: Wire up notification click handler in background.ts
userStories.description: Call initNotificationClickHandler() from background.ts at startup so notification click events are handled. Add unit tests for the click handler.
acceptanceCriteria: background.ts imports and calls initNotificationClickHandler() at startup
Clicking a notification with a stored URL opens that URL in a new tab
Clicking a notification without a URL opens the side panel
The notification is cleared after being clicked
Unit tests in notification-commands.test.ts cover click-to-open-url and click-to-open-side-panel scenarios
Unit tests verify notification cleanup on click
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts (add init call)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/notification-commands.ts (verify initNotificationClickHandler export)
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/notification-commands.test.ts (add click handler tests)

Context:
In background.ts, `initConfirmationBadge()` is already called at startup. Search for it — add `initNotificationClickHandler()` right after it:
```typescript
initConfirmationBadge();
initNotificationClickHandler();
```

The click handler in notification-commands.ts should:
1. Register a `chrome.notifications.onClicked` listener
2. Check if the notificationId starts with 'opentabs-notify-'
3. If it has a stored URL in the module-level Map, call `chrome.tabs.create({ url })` and remove the entry from the Map
4. If no URL, open the side panel via `chrome.windows.getCurrent()` then `chrome.sidePanel.open({ windowId })`
5. Always call `chrome.notifications.clear(notificationId)`

The existing `initConfirmationBadge()` in confirmation-badge.ts (line 111-125) already registers an `onClicked` listener that filters for `notificationId === 'opentabs-confirmation'`. Both listeners will fire for every click, but each filters by its own ID prefix, so they don't conflict.

For unit tests, mock `chrome.notifications.onClicked.addListener`, `chrome.tabs.create`, `chrome.sidePanel.open`, `chrome.windows.getCurrent`, and `chrome.notifications.clear`. Call `initNotificationClickHandler()`, capture the listener callback, and invoke it with test notification IDs.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify initConfirmationBadge or confirmation-badge.ts
- The onClicked listener MUST only handle notifications with the 'opentabs-notify-' prefix
